summary: No-API answer-quality review for qv6_010 generic title ambiguity: L38 is safer than L37 because it records the expected abstention, but it still over-answers by appending a long candidate requirements summary after saying the identifier is insufficient.

models.review_method: Manual review of saved local JSON and read-only source context only; no paid APIs called.

outputs.description: proposal contract for qv6_010 ambiguity-overanswer scoring rule

case_under_review.expected_behavior: The title fragment alone is non-identifying. A correct answer should refuse to bind to a single project and ask for additional identifiers such as issuer, notice number, publication date, or full title.
case_under_review.why_it_matters: Generic RFP titles like an integrated information-system advancement project can match multiple issuers. A retrieved candidate may be relevant-looking, but the answer should not turn insufficient identification into a long factual project summary.

l37_before.observed_behavior: L37 binds the generic fragment to retrieved candidate documents and provides a detailed requirements/scope answer. Because the case has no target_orgs and expects abstention, this is an under-refusal failure.

l38_after.observed_behavior: L38 opens with the right safety move: the title alone cannot identify one project and additional identifiers are needed. However, it then appends a long candidate summary with project general information, implementation scope, requirement categories, performance/interface/data/security examples, submission/evaluation notes, and follow-up instructions.

before_after_quality_comparison.net_assessment: L38 fixes the binary abstention metric but not the user-facing answer quality. It should receive abstention credit, but not full quality credit, because the long candidate summary can look like an endorsed answer after a refusal.

proposed_failure_label.label: ambiguous_identifier_refusal_with_excessive_candidate_summary
proposed_failure_label.definition: The answer correctly states that the user supplied insufficient identifiers or an ambiguous/generic title fragment, but then provides a long factual summary of one or more retrieved candidate projects beyond minimal disambiguation metadata.
proposed_failure_label.distinguish_from_under_refusal: L37 is under_refusal because it does not abstain. L38 is not under_refusal; it is a post-refusal over-answer because the refusal is present but diluted by extensive candidate content.

detector_and_scoring_rule.name: post_abstention_candidate_summary_penalty
detector_and_scoring_rule.scope: Apply only when expect_abstention is true or when the answer contains an ambiguity/insufficient-identifier refusal lead.
trigger_conditions: answer is classified as abstention true
the first paragraph says the title/fragment/request is insufficient, ambiguous, non-identifying, or needs additional identifiers
after that refusal, the answer contains extended candidate content
extended_candidate_content_heuristics: more than 700 characters after the initial refusal paragraph
more than 5 bullets or more than 3 section headers after the refusal
mentions detailed requirement categories such as performance, interface, data, security, warranty, submission, evaluation, or implementation scope
contains multiple document citations attached to candidate requirements rather than only candidate identity anchors
lists budgets, periods, requirement IDs, or operational constraints for a candidate after saying the identifier is insufficient
scoring_effect: Keep abstention_accuracy credit because the model did refuse.
For exposed-set review, cap the case quality at partial/pass_with_issue rather than clean pass.
For report-ready promotion, treat this as a blocker until the answer is shortened or the user explicitly asks for possible candidates.
detector_and_scoring_rule.minimal_formula: If expect_abstention && abstention && post_refusal_detail_score >= 2, mark issue major and subtract a small fixed quality penalty; if no refusal, keep the existing under_refusal/abstention failure.

prompt_change_suggestion.suggested_rule: When the supplied title fragment or generic title cannot identify one project, answer in at most 3 short bullets: (1) say the identifier is insufficient, (2) name the extra identifiers needed, and (3) optionally list only candidate identity anchors, limited to issuer/title pairs. Do not summarize requirements, budgets, scopes, schedules, or document details until the user selects a candidate or supplies an identifier.
prompt_change_suggestion.why_narrow: This does not suppress useful disambiguation. It only prevents long factual summaries after the model has already admitted that the query is ambiguous.

risk_of_over_penalizing_useful_disambigu.risk: A helpful answer may need to show possible candidates so the user can choose the right one.
mitigation: Allow a short candidate list with issuer, exact title, and maybe document slot references.
Do not penalize candidate identity anchors when no requirement, budget, schedule, or scope details are included.
Do not apply the penalty when the user explicitly asks for candidate comparison or says to list all possible matches.
Treat 1-3 short candidate bullets as allowed disambiguation, not over-answering.
risk_of_over_penalizing_useful_disambigu.allowed_example_shape: I cannot identify a single project from this fragment. Please provide issuer/notice number/date/full title. Possible candidates in the retrieved set are: issuer A - title X; issuer B - title Y.

steps: Run a local heuristic pass over saved L37 and L38 details.json for qv6_010 and qv6_004 only.
Compute answer length after the first refusal paragraph, bullet count after refusal, and whether candidate details include requirement/scope/budget/schedule/security tokens.

Expected result: L37 is under_refusal; L38 qv6_010 is abstention_correct_but_overlong; L38 qv6_004 should remain acceptable if its post-refusal content is limited to requested missing fields and official document distinctions.
Add one or two future frozen negative controls with generic titles and multiple retrieved issuers, but do not call a paid judge until the local detector has been reviewed.
next_minimal_no_or_low_cost_test.cost: No paid API required; uses saved details.json and deterministic text heuristics.

implementation_notes_for_later_owner: No protected files were changed by this worker.
A later approved source change could add this as an extra issue label in evaluation aggregation without changing binary abstention_accuracy.

Because scripts/evaluate.py currently classifies abstention as a boolean, this proposal should be implemented as a separate qualitative issue label or report-ready gate rather than by redefining abstention itself.

observed_results: qv6_010 expects abstention because the generic fragment alone must not force an arbitrary issuer.
L37 qv6_010 has abstention=false while expect_abstention=true, and provides a detailed candidate answer.
L38 qv6_010 has abstention=true while expect_abstention=true, but still includes a long candidate requirements summary after the ambiguity refusal.
src/generator.py already contains a broad title-fragment ambiguity rule, but it does not explicitly cap post-refusal candidate detail.
scripts/evaluate.py uses binary abstention detection; the proposed rule is therefore an additional quality issue, not a replacement for abstention detection.
No paid APIs were called and outputs were confined to the two allowed worker_outputs files.
